decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500 W/mK), and geometry changes are constrained by legality limits. The most effective remaining lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve heat extraction from the spreader to the cooled boundaries. We will increase base_material.conductivity from 12.0 to 24.0 (2x step, within bounds) to enhance downstream thermal transport.

changes.reason: Increasing base plate conductivity improves the thermal path from the high-conductivity spreader to the Dirichlet boundaries on the left and right, reducing the overall thermal resistance and lowering the chip peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
Improved lateral and vertical heat flux through the base layer
More uniform temperature distribution in the base plate
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts entirely to the spreader-to-base interface or if the boundary conditions limit further extraction.
Large jumps in conductivity can sometimes cause solver convergence issues, though LU solver is generally robust for steady-state linear problems.
